cooperates.cooperate_desc: Minsitthar and Vexana establish a formidable crowd control engine designed to systematically lock down and eliminate enemy targets. Their synergy relies on a "chain cc" strategy where Minsittar's ability to group and ground opponents creates a guaranteed opening for vexana's high impact magical burst.

counters.counter_desc: While Sora can bypass the constraints of King's Calling through the unique displacement of his Thunder Form, Minsitthar remains a potent counter by obstructing the dashes necessary for him to accumulate stacks.  By suppressing his mobility, Minsitthar interrupts Sora's momentum and creates a critical window to secure a kill before he's able to achieve maximum stacks to activate his thunder form ultimate. This counter effect requires precise timing and advanced mechanical skill; deploying the ultimate prematurely allows Sora an opportunity to escape, whereas a perfectly timed engagement ensures he remains trapped and unable to cycle his abilities

Belerick stands as one of the most formidable counters to Minsitthar due to his innate ability to punish high-frequency basic attacks. Since Minsitthar's effectiveness relies on rapid    attack speed generated by his core items to trigger his passive, Belerick's deadly thorns becomes a constant threat. Every strike minsitthar lands has a chance to trigger Belerick's passive.

The core of this counter relationship lies in Karina's first skill, Dance of Blades. When activated, Karina becomes immune to all incoming basic attacks for a brief duration. Because Minsitthar relies on high attack speed and successive hits to stack his passive, Mark of the King, Karina can effectively negate his entire offensive cycle. While she is in this state, Minsitthar cannot apply stacks, meaning he cannot trigger his crucial stun or the 400 HP restorative heal, leaving him vulnerable to her high magic burst.

Akai is a direct counter because of his ultimate, Heavy spin, completely ruins Minsittar's setup. Minsitthar relies on keeping enemies inside his grounded zone (King's calling) to enable his royal guards and prevent escapes. Akai can simply activate his ultimate and push Minsitthar out of his own circle, rendering the "grounded" effect useless for the rest of the team.  Furthermore, because Akai's spin provide cc immunity and displacements, he can pin Minsitthar against a wall, forcing him to stop attacking and preventing him from stacking his passive.

Diggie is widely considered Minsitthar's hardest counter in a team fight scenario. Minsitthar's strength lies in trapping multiple enemies and disabling their mobility; however, Diggie's ultimate, Time Journey, provides an immediate AoE (Area of Effect) cleanse and a massive shield to all nearby allies.

Minsitthar is arguably the strongest counter to Benedetta due to her total reliance on dash mechanics. Nearly every aspects of Benedetta's skills including her basic attacks is considered a dash or blink.
By grounding her, Minsitthar turns a high-mobility slaughter into a stationary fight where his team can easily collapse on her and burst her down before she can leap away.

Arlott's entire identity revolves around his ability to dash repeatedly toward targets marked by his "Demon Gaze." Inside Minsitthar's ultimate, King's Calling, Arlott is completely grounded, rendering his second skill unusable.
By removing the Chrono Dash, Minsitthar strips Harith of his damage, his defense (shields), and his escape, making him one of the most reliable picks to shut down a Harith carry composition.
Minsitthar renders Ling's entire sword cycling mechanic impossible. However this requires great timing possibly after Ling's ultimate so he doesn't use it to escape instead.

dominants.dominant_desc: Minsitthar's dominance is primarily realised in the mid to late game, once he has gotten his attack speed items. During the early laning phase, his solo kill potential is limited; focus should remain on defensive play and lane management. Utilize Spear of Glory to displace overextended enemies into the turret's range to secure early advantages. It is vital to reach level four before the first turtle contest, as King's calling is a decisive objective control tool. During team fights, prioritize the enemy jungler with your ultimate to neutralize their Retribution and escape potential. Avoid expending high value crowd control on the enemy tank; instead, coordinate with your team to follow up on your initiation. As the match progresses into the late game, Minsittar's damage scaling allows him to secure solo kills. At this stage, your primary focus should be target locking high mobility heroes, effectively removing their tactical advantage from the battlefield.

emblems.emblem_desc:
Swift: Increases attack speed to rapidly trigger the five-stack passive, ensuring consistent stuns and health recovery during trades.
Master Assassin: Grants a 7% damage boost in isolated 1v1 encounters, maximizing kill potential after pulling a target with Spear of Glory.
Quantum Charge: Provides a 30% movement speed burst and HP restoration upon dealing basic attack damage, granting Minsitthar the necessary kiting and repositioning tools he naturally lacks.

equips.equip_desc: The core of this build centers on a high atrack speed to maximise minsitthar's dueling potential through rapid on hit edfects. Corrosion Scythe serves as the initial foundation, providing the necessary slow and speed to control the laning phase and stick to targets. Demon Hunter Sword is integrated to provide percentage-based damage, which scales effectively against high-health opponents. Golden Staff acts as the central multiplier of the build; it converts critical chance into additional attack speed and enables every third basic attack to trigger item and passive effects multiple times. This synergy ensures that Minsitthar can detonate his Mark of the King passive almost instantly, resulting in constant stuns and health restoration that allows him to overwhelm enemies in prolonged engagements.

hero.hero_overview: Minsitthar is a versatile Fighter/Controller primarily utilized in the EXP Lane. He possesses a unique utility profile; beyond standard immobilization, his crowd control specifically suppresses dash and blink mechanics, effectively neutralizing high mobility opponents.
hero.hero_strength: His ultimate, King's calling, serves as a premier counter for high mobility heroes by effectively preventing all blinks and dashes within the ultimate. Furthermore, his passive, Mark of the King, provides a significant sustain boost of 400 HP upon reaching maximum stacks. This mechanical synergy allows Minsitthar to bait aggressive plays, turning a perceived vulnerability into a lethal counter-ambush once the heal and stun are triggered.
hero.hero_weakness: Minsitthar is notably susceptible to continuous crowd control and displacements, particularly from heroes like Grock or Akai, who can destroy his positioning and ability casting. His heavy reliance on basic attack to trigger his passive makes him Vulnerable to heroes like belerick; the combination of Belerick's passive, vengeance and blade armour effectively turns Minsittar's offensive speed into a self destructive liability. Furthermore, heroes with basic attack negation or reflection such as karina, can neutralise his primary source of damage and passive accumulation or maximisation. His tactical value is also significantly reduced by the support diggie whose ultimate cab cleanse minsitthar's ultimate effect.

data.recommend: A professional masterclass on Minsitthar. Optimize your attack-speed builds and learn advanced suppression tactics to shut down high-mobility assassins and dominate the current competitive meta.

spell.spell_desc: While high-mobility heroes typically excel at evasion, Minsitthar can effectively neutralize this advantage through the strategic use of Flicker to initiate surprise engagements. Similar to Franco, Minsitthar can execute a "Flicker-hook" mechanic to significantly extend the displacement range of his first skill, pulling targets into unfavorable positions or under friendly turrets. Furthermore, if a suppressed target survives the duration of the ultimate, Flicker serves as a vital tool to safely disengage and reset the encounter, compensating for Minsitthar's inherent lack of natural mobility.

hero.hero_overview: Minsitthar in Mobile Legends is a tank-fighter known for his crowd control and team-fight presence, best suited for the EXP lane. His ultimate skill, which restricts enemies from using mobility skills, makes him a strong initiator.

spell.spell_desc: a battle spell that enhances a hero's basic attacks, increasing attack speed and lifesteal for a short duration. It's particularly useful for Marksmen (MMs) who rely on basic attacks for damage. The spell also ignores a portion of the target's physical defense and provides HP restoration on each hit.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Reason: Lylia, with her ability to deal significant damage from a distance, is an excellent partner for Minstitthar. When Minstitthar uses his skill Minstitthar's Rage to group enemies together, Lylia can use her Black Shoes ability to attack the gathered enemies with her powerful magical abilities, dealing massive area damage. Additionally, Lylia is quick to escape thanks to her mobility, making her the perfect partner for Minstitthar, who may need some support in battles.

counters.counter_desc: Reason: Roger is a strong offensive hero with Wolf Transformation, allowing him to deal quick damage. Paired with Minstitthar, Roger benefits from Minstitthar's crowd control to set up attacks. When enemies are immobilized, Roger can transform into a Wolf and deal heavy damage, complementing Minstitthar's direct combat style.

dominants.dominant_desc: Before reaching level 4 to unlock the ultimate skill Minstitthar's Rage, Minstitthar should focus on farming and playing defensively. Use his basic skills like Thrust to knock back and control enemies, and Justice Strike to deal damage. It's best to play defensively, move carefully with your team, and use the Flicker spell for escapes or surprise attacks. Coordinate with your team to take down jungle monsters and control lanes, allowing you to build strength in preparation for unlocking the ultimate skill.

emblems.emblem_desc: Increase damage, while basic attacks also slow enemies by 50% and reduce their attack speed by 30%.

equips.equip_desc: The best equipment for Minstitthar in Mobile Legends focuses on enhancing his durability and increasing his damage output in battles. Blade of Despair boosts his attack damage, while Cursed Helmet provides magic resistance and continuous damage to nearby enemies. Dominance Ice increases his defense and slows down enemies, while Immortality grants him a second life after death. Antique Cuirass enhances his durability against basic attacks, and Warrior Boots / Tough Boots offer protection against physical or magic damage. These items make Minstitthar powerful in team fights.

hero.hero_overview: Minstitthar is a fighter hero in Mobile Legends, renowned for his high durability and powerful combat abilities, making him one of the most formidable front-line warriors. His background is rooted in ancient wars, where he trained to become a legendary fighter. In the game, his primary role is to dominate close-range battles, control enemy movements, and provide support in team fights. To effectively develop the hero, focus on equipping items that boost his power and survivability, while carefully timing his skills for control and engagements.
hero.hero_strength: Minstitthar is a strong hero in team fights thanks to his ability to control enemies and restore his health during battles. In the mid and late game, he becomes even stronger, able to absorb significant damage and play a crucial role in team fights. His skills, such as Minstitthar's Rage and Thrust, make him ideal for controlling enemies and creating opportunities for kills. With his high durability and flexibility in combat, Minstitthar is a powerful strategic choice throughout all stages of the game.
hero.hero_weakness: Despite Minstitthar's strength in team fights and his high durability, he has some drawbacks. He is weak against long-range heroes and lacks high mobility, making him vulnerable to attacks or easy to escape from. He also heavily relies on the timing of his skills, and if not used properly, he may lose his impact in battle. Additionally, he lacks crowd control removal abilities, making him susceptible to being disabled by enemies.

spell.spell_desc: The Flicker spell with Minstitthar offers great advantages in battle. It can be used to escape dangerous situations or chase down fleeing enemies. It also helps in surprising opponents by attacking from unexpected angles and enhances coordination with his skills like Thrust and Minstitthar's Rage, boosting his ability to control the fight.

cooperates.cooperate_desc: Minsithar works very well before or after carmillias ultimate being able to keep the enemy team from running due to his anti dash ability and because his stun will become a never ending cc chain
Death car hit something and it will probably die it is not that good cause Johnson is bad so it kinda kills the rest of the team
Cc go brrr

counters.counter_desc: He is generally good against assasins due to his ulti and burst I put haya here but any assassin is weak to him except Natalia
Because Khaleed heavily rely on dashes minsithar ulti is able to trap him but he is unable to kill Kahleed due to his durability
Similar to Kahleed
Similar to Kahleed but Benedetta has low durability and can be easily killed in minsithar ulti
Same as bene
Generally anyone that does not rely on dash and just burst out ridiculous damage

dominants.dominant_desc: Wait for enemies to use skills before diving in and attacking the enemy back off after triggering passive unless killing the enemy is possible refrain from overextending since minsithar is bad at surviving ganks

Lock down the enemy hyper with his ulti if they are assasins and try to kill then to guarantee hyper take the turtle if enemies pick tanky hyper use minsithar passive and ulti to slow and stun them to prevent the enemy from being able to retri

Minsithar has low durability and should avoid absorbing to much damage use his second skill to safely poke from a distance and reduce damage taken and also use his first skill to pull in enemy marksman or mages due to its unique property to bypass enemy frontline

When pushing turrets minsithar second skill does not deal additional damage so you can save mana by not using them however it would trigger trinity effects faster if u use it to hit the minions 
Do not be afraid to go in as his passive can heal and make him be able to hold out and not die easily 
When losing use his hook to pull out enemy and isolate enemies to kill them in order to get their bounty and equal out the game
dominants.dominant_title: Mistakes I made when starting out

emblems.emblem_desc: It is essential that minsithar can win his early game as he rely on his attack speed to trigger his passive and sustain himself quantum charge is also a way to compensate for his lack of mobility and can be swapped out with weakness finder against other attack speed exp heros
Easy assassination and burst build

equips.equip_desc: Minsithar can win early fights by utilizing his passive to consistently do damage in the early game and sustain himself  allowing him to sustain himself and slowly wear down the enemy I recommend this build against tanky or high hp a emu due to its hp dependent damage
This build is better for squishier enemies with its high burst damage but lack of def penetration and hp dependent damage

hero.hero_overview: A crazy good hero to start with because of his flexibility to cover gold exp and roam I am creating this guide so people can learn about things I wish I new when I first started he is also rising in wr making this the best time to learn and use him
hero.hero_strength: Minsithar has a rather fast clear speed both for minion waves and turret making it easy to farm and get fed with him ,he is also very strong in teamfight due to his ability to zone with his ultimate and stun multiple enemies with his second skill not having many counters is also his strength making him a safe first pick as most teams have dashes that minsithar can reliably counter
hero.hero_weakness: Minsithar is a generally good hero regardless of the stage of the game however he has some weaknesses regardless of the strategy used like struggling with pushing due to his low mobility and inability to run when getting ganked or during team fights where his effectiveness drops when against a single target however it is also his strength because if you learn to remedy his weaknesses either with a build or strategy  he can be very strong

data.recommend: Good for all stages of the game from an experienced player

spell.spell_desc: As said earlier his skills trade mobility for damage and cc flicker helps compensate for that issue with his ulti and hook being compatible with the flicker to do surprise moves or target backline inspire is also viable but is very matchup dependent
